detail: No direct subprocess calls. Uses pytubefix and faster-whisper as libraries. CUDA toolkit path discovery via glob is safe. No shell injection vectors.
Zero test files. No testing framework. Single Python file.
No CI/CD. Ruff configured in pyproject.toml but no enforcement pipeline.
Single monolithic file (~280 lines). All config as module-level variables. No classes, no separation of concerns. Functions are well-named but everything in one file.
Dead imports (ProcessPoolExecutor, fastapi in requirements). No abstraction — download, transcribe, NLP, output all in one flow. Not designed for reuse.
No extension points. Config via hard-coded variables at file top. No CLI args, no env vars, no config file.
OpenAI API key as hard-coded string with placeholder. No .env support, no secrets manager.
clean_filename sanitizes titles for filesystem use. Directory creation with exist_ok. But no path traversal guards on downloaded content.
No type annotations except one function signature. No mypy config. Ruff ignores line length.
requirements.txt with 11 unpinned deps. Dead dep (fastapi). pyproject.toml has ruff config only. Python 3.12 specified via .python-version.
Try/except around download with traceback.print_exc. Returns None on download failure. But transcription errors would crash — no recovery for mid-playlist failures.
Extensive README with functional overview, implementation details, and Whisper model config. No inline code docs beyond variable names.
